inputs.reasoning: Alternation (kInstAlt) with repetition forces the switch(arg) cases in kInstAlt handling, exercising the arg==0 and arg==1 branches.
Nested repetitions create complex NFA structures that exercise the TrySearch loop and CheckAndLoop label path through the state machine.
Character class with byte range matching exercises kInstByteRange case and the if(p < end) condition for reading input characters.
Named capture group exercises kInstCapture case with arg==0 branch and cap() register handling.
Multiple sequential captures create many Push operations, potentially exercising stack growth and njob_ counter increments.
Large counted repetition with wide range creates many instruction states, stressing the job stack and potentially triggering GrowStack when njob_ >= maxjob_.
Multiple alternations exercise kInstAltMatch case and the greedy() branch decision logic.
Multiple optional segments with the ShouldVisit check in the CheckAndLoop label, exercising state visit tracking.
Negated character class with byte range matching exercises the Matches() condition and character reading at boundary.
Nested groups with repetition and alternation creates complex state transitions exercising the main TrySearch while loop.
